title: Initial urgent care presentation
scenarioBrief: Maria Sandoval, a 22-year-old Spanish-speaking woman, was seen at urgent care today (2026-01-12) for 6 months of progressive joint pain and hair loss. You receive her urgent care records and a request to follow up. Review the chart and respond to the prompts below. You may use the AI assistant freely.

chartGate._notes: AP1 visibility: pre-immigration history, 2018-2019 community health visits, 2026-01-12 urgent care visit + its labs. No further encounters yet.

prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2 content session: write the prompt asking for a top differential diagnosis. Should encourage use of AI assistant.

essential: TODO: list ~5 essential DDx items the resident should generate (e.g., SLE, viral arthropathy, RA, UCTD, reactive arthritis)
bonus: TODO: list 3-5 bonus items showing broader thinking (e.g., autoimmune thyroiditis, disseminated TB given epidemiology, Behcet's, Sjogren's)
redFlags: TODO: list reasoning errors that should be penalized (e.g., anchoring on SLE based on 1:80 ANA alone, ignoring the empiric HCQ-before-specialist-eval problem)

prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2: write the prompt asking the resident to describe which chart sections they fed the AI and why.

essential: TODO: essential chart elements the resident should include (HPI, FHx, social Hx with immigration context)
bonus: TODO: bonus context choices showing nuance (explicit ANA titer with reference range, recognition that med list does not bias DDx)
redFlags: TODO: red flags (dumping entire chart, including PAT001 data accidentally)

prompts._aiPromptToGenerate: TODO — Phase 2: write the system prompt that the assessment engine will use to generate a deliberately-flawed AI sample output for the resident to critique. Should anchor on SLE, miss viral arthropathy and the disseminated TB consideration, be confidently worded.
prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2: write the prompt asking the resident to evaluate the AI response shown above.

shouldIdentify: TODO: items the resident should flag (missing viral arthropathy, over-confident in SLE, not addressing thyroid finding, not considering TB)
shouldNotEcho: TODO: things the resident should NOT just parrot back (the AI's exact wording, false certainty)
